A French girl asks: "What series and music do you love? Tell me everything!" Talk about your top 3 favorites and why. Use love, like, hate, obsessed.
Sugestão de resposta
“I am obsessed with Gossip Girl. I love Blair Waldorf because she is smart and stylish. I also love Friends — it is so funny. I do not like horror movies, but I love romantic comedies. What about you?”

Survival Card — Lesson 5: Do You Like It?

1I love Gossip Girl.Eu adoro Gossip Girl.
2I don't like horror movies.Eu não gosto de filmes de terror.
3I am obsessed with Friends.Sou obcecada por Friends.
4My favorite character is Blair.Meu personagem favorito é a Blair.
5What about you? What do you like?E você? Do que você gosta?
Aula 06 — Pre-class

Numbers, Time & Dates — The Tools You Need to Survive in Paris

Depois desta aula, você consegue perguntar as horas, entender horários de voo e comprar ingressos em Paris sem travar.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de dizer as horas, perguntar preços e dizer datas em inglês — tudo que precisa para sobreviver em Paris.

Vocabulário Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

O’clock
em ponto
“It is three o’clock.”
Quarter
quinze minutos (quarto de hora)
“It is a quarter past two.”
Half past
meia hora (e meia)
“It is half past ten.”
Noon
meio-dia
“We have lunch at noon.”
Midnight
meia-noite
“The flight arrives at midnight.”
Flight
voo
“My flight is at nine o’clock.”
Ticket
passagem / ingresso
“How much is the ticket?”
Price
preço
“What is the price?”

V2. Match the words Matching

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a explicação com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

O’clock
Quarter
Half past
Noon
Midnight
Flight
Ticket
Price

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context Grammar

Leia o texto com calma. As palavras em negrito são do vocabulário de hoje.

Gabriela is planning her trip to Paris. Her flight is on February 3rd, 2027. The departure time is nine fifteen in the morning. She arrives in Paris at half past eleven at night. On February 4th, she visits the Louvre at ten o’clock. Lunch is at half past twelve at a French café. The Eiffel Tower visit is at a quarter past four. A ticket to the Eiffel Tower costs twenty-six euros. Gabriela asks: “What time is it?” and “How much is the price?”
What time does Gabriela visit the Louvre?
A At half past ten
B At ten o’clock
C At a quarter past ten
How much is the Eiffel Tower ticket?
A Fifteen euros
B Thirty euros
C Twenty-six euros
What time is the Eiffel Tower visit?
A A quarter past four
B Half past four
C Four o’clock

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip Grammar

Telling Time in American English / Dizendo as horas em inglês americano

3:00It is three o’clock.São três horas.
3:15It is a quarter past three.São três e quinze.
3:30It is half past three.São três e meia.
3:45It is a quarter to four.São quinze para as quatro.
12:00 PMIt is noon.É meio-dia.
12:00 AMIt is midnight.É meia-noite.

Dates in American English / Datas em inglês americano

In American English, the format is: Month + Day (ordinal) + Year.
Exemplo: February 3rd, 2027 (não “3 February”).
Ordinals: 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th... 15th... 21st, 22nd, 23rd... 31st.

Questions / Perguntas:
“What time is it?” — Que horas são?
“How much is it?” / “How much does this cost?” — Quanto custa?
“When is your flight?” — Quando é o seu voo?

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma.

“Excuse me, what is it?”
“My is on February 3rd.”
“How much is the ?”
“It is ten.”
“The museum opens at ten .”

P2. Put in order Order

Coloque as frases na ordem correta para formar um diálogo no guichê do Louvre.

? “It is 15 euros. Here is your ticket.”
? “We close at six o’clock. Enjoy your visit!”
? “Good afternoon! One student ticket, please.”
? “Thank you! What time does the museum close?”
? “How much is it?”

S1. Read aloud Speaking

Toque em Ouvir, repita, depois grave e compare. O objetivo é praticar, não ser perfeito.

Excuse me, what time is it?
My flight is on February 3rd, 2027.
One ticket, please. How much is it?
The museum opens at ten o’clock.

Q1. Choose the best answer Quiz

Imagine a situação e escolha a melhor resposta.

You are at the Louvre and want to buy a ticket. What do you say?
A “I want ticket Louvre.”
B “One ticket, please. How much is it?”
C “Give me one ticket now.”
You need to know the time. What do you ask?
A “What is the hour?”
B “Tell me the clock.”
C “Excuse me, what time is it?”
The clock shows 4:45. How do you say it?
A “It is a quarter to five.”
B “It is a quarter past four.”
C “It is half past four.”
Someone asks: “When is your flight?” What do you answer?
A “My flight is 3 February.”
B “My flight is on February 3rd.”
C “Flight February three.”

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

You are at the Eiffel Tower ticket counter in Paris. Tell the person: your name, when your flight arrived, ask for one ticket, ask the price, and ask what time the tower closes.
Sugestão de resposta
“Hi, my name is Gabriela. My flight arrived on February 3rd. One ticket, please. How much is it? What time does the tower close?”

Survival Card — Lesson 6: Numbers, Time & Dates

1Excuse me, what time is it?Com licença, que horas são?
2My flight is on February 3rd.Meu voo é dia 3 de fevereiro.
3One ticket, please. How much is it?Uma entrada, por favor. Quanto custa?
4The museum opens at ten o’clock.O museu abre às dez horas.
5It is half past twelve.É meio-dia e meia.
Aula 07 — Pre-class

Where Are You From? — Countries, Nationalities & the World Around Gabriela

Depois desta aula, você consegue se apresentar dizendo de onde é, sua nacionalidade e que idiomas fala — tudo que precisa ao conhecer viajantes em Paris.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de dizer de onde é, perguntar a nacionalidade dos outros e falar sobre idiomas — perfeito para o hostel em Paris.

Vocabulário Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

Country
país
“Brazil is a big country.”
Nationality
nacionalidade
“What is your nationality?”
Brazilian
brasileiro/a
“I am Brazilian.”
French
francês/francesa
“She is French.”
American
americano/a
“Blake Lively is American.”
Language
idioma / língua
“I speak two languages.”
Passport
passaporte
“My passport is Brazilian.”
International
internacional
“Paris is an international city.”

V2. Match the words Matching

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a explicação com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

Country
Nationality
Brazilian
French
American
Language
Passport
International

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context Grammar

Leia o texto com calma. As palavras em negrito são do vocabulário de hoje.

Gabriela is from Brazil. She is Brazilian. She speaks Portuguese and she is learning English. At the hostel in Paris, she meets Liam. He is from Australia. He is Australian. He speaks English and a little French. There is also Yuki. She is from Japan. She is Japanese. The hostel is very international — there are people from ten different countries! Gabriela shows her passport at the reception. “What nationality?” asks the receptionist. “I am Brazilian,” says Gabriela. “Welcome! You speak good English!” “Thank you! I am learning. What languages do people speak here?” “English, French, Japanese, Italian... It is truly international!”
Where is Liam from?
A France
B Australia
C Japan
What is Gabriela’s nationality?
A French
B American
C Brazilian
How is the hostel described?
A International
B Small
C French only

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip Grammar

Talking About Origin & Nationality / Falando sobre origem e nacionalidade

PerguntaWhere are you from?De onde você é?
PaísI am from Brazil.Eu sou do Brasil.
NacionalidadeI am Brazilian.Eu sou brasileira.
3a pessoaShe is French. / He is from Australia.Ela é francesa. / Ele é da Austrália.
IdiomaI speak Portuguese and English.Eu falo português e inglês.
PluralThey are from Japan.Eles são do Japão.

Countries & Nationalities / Países e nacionalidades

CountryNationalityCelebridade
BrazilBrazilianGabriela!
FranceFrenchLily Collins (Emily in Paris)
USAAmericanBlake Lively (Gossip Girl)
EnglandEnglishEd Westwick (Gossip Girl)
AustraliaAustralianChris Hemsworth
JapanJapaneseMarie Kondo
ItalyItalianMonica Bellucci
CanadaCanadianRyan Reynolds

Dica importante: Em inglês, nacionalidades NÃO mudam para feminino/masculino. “Brazilian” = ele E ela. Diferente do português (brasileiro/brasileira).

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma.

“Where are you ?”
“I am .”
“Blake Lively is .”
“I speak two .”
“My is Brazilian.”

P2. Put in order Order

Coloque as frases na ordem correta para formar uma conversa no hostel.

? “I am from Sydney. And you?”
? “I am from Australia!”
? “I am from São Paulo, Brazil!”
? “Hi! Where are you from?”
? “Cool! Where in Australia?”

S1. Read aloud Speaking

Toque em Ouvir, repita, depois grave e compare. O objetivo é praticar, não ser perfeito.

Where are you from?
I am from São Paulo, Brazil.
I am Brazilian. I speak Portuguese.
I speak Portuguese and I am learning English.

Q1. Choose the best answer Quiz

Imagine a situação e escolha a melhor resposta.

Someone at the hostel asks: “Where are you from?” What do you say?
A “I from Brazil.”
B “I am from Brazil.”
C “My from is Brazil.”
You want to know someone’s nationality. What do you ask?
A “What country you?”
B “Where are you from?”
C “You are from where?”
Ed Westwick is from England. What is his nationality?
A American
B English
C Australian
Someone says: “I speak Japanese and English.” They are probably from...
A France
B Japan
C Italy

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

You arrive at a youth hostel in Paris. Introduce yourself to a new traveler. Say: your name, where you are from, your nationality, what languages you speak, and how long you are in Paris.
Sugestão de resposta
“Hi, I am Gabriela. I am from São Paulo, Brazil. I am Brazilian. I speak Portuguese and I am learning English. My passport is Brazilian. I am in Paris for two weeks!”

Survival Card — Lesson 7: Countries & Nationalities

1Where are you from?De onde você é?
2I am from São Paulo, Brazil.Eu sou de São Paulo, Brasil.
3I am Brazilian. I speak Portuguese.Eu sou brasileira. Eu falo português.
4What is your nationality?Qual é a sua nacionalidade?
5This city is very international!Esta cidade é muito internacional!
Aula 08 — Pre-class

Can You Do That? — Abilities, Possibilities & Asking for Help

Depois desta aula, você consegue pedir ajuda, dizer o que pode e não pode fazer, e sobreviver em situações de emergência em Paris.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de usar “can” e “can’t” para falar sobre habilidades, pedir ajuda e usar frases de sobrevivência em Paris.

Vocabulário Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

Speak
falar
“I can speak English.”
Understand
entender
“I can understand you.”
Help
ajudar
“Can you help me?”
Repeat
repetir
“Can you repeat that, please?”
Find
encontrar
“I can’t find my hotel.”
Show
mostrar
“Can you show me on the map?”
Slower
mais devagar
“Can you speak slower, please?”
Map
mapa
“Do you have a map?”

V2. Match the words Matching

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a explicação com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

Speak
Understand
Help
Repeat
Find
Show
Slower
Map

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context Grammar

Leia o texto com calma. As palavras em negrito são do vocabulário de hoje.

Gabriela is walking in Paris. It is her second day. She can speak English, but she can’t speak French. She is lost! She can’t find her hotel. She sees a man near the metro. She asks: “Can you help me?” The man says: “Yes, I can!” Gabriela says: “I can’t find my hotel. Can you show me on the map?” The man speaks fast. Gabriela says: “Can you speak slower, please?” He repeats slowly. Now Gabriela can understand! She says: “Thank you! I can find it now!”
Can Gabriela speak French?
A Yes, she can.
B No, she can’t.
C She speaks a little French.
What can’t Gabriela find?
A The metro station
B Her hotel
C A restaurant
What does Gabriela ask the man to do?
A Walk with her to the hotel
B Show her on the map and speak slower
C Call a taxi

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip Grammar

Modal Verb CAN / O verbo modal CAN

AfirmativaI can speak English.Eu sei/consigo falar inglês.
NegativaI can’t find my hotel.Eu não consigo encontrar meu hotel.
PerguntaCan you help me?Você pode me ajudar?
Resp. curta (+)Yes, I can.Sim, eu posso.
Resp. curta (−)No, I can’t.Não, eu não posso.
Pedido educadoCould you help me, please?Você poderia me ajudar, por favor?

Regras importantes / Important rules

  • CAN + verbo BASE (sem “to”!): I can speak — NUNCA “I can to speak”
  • CAN não muda com he/she: She can swim (nunca “she cans”)
  • CAN’T = cannot (pronúncia: /kænt/ — vogal curta)
  • COULD = mais educado que CAN para pedidos
  • Pergunta: CAN + sujeito + verbo (Can you help me?)

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma.

“I speak English.”
“I find my hotel.”
“Can you me?”
“Can you speak , please?”
“Can you me on the map?”

P2. Put in order Order

Coloque as frases na ordem correta para formar uma conversa pedindo ajuda.

? “I can’t find my hotel. Can you help me?”
? “Can you speak slower, please?”
? “Excuse me, can you help me?”
? “Yes, I can. It is near the metro. Walk straight.”
? “Yes, of course! What is the problem?”

S1. Read aloud Speaking

Toque em Ouvir, repita, depois grave e compare. O objetivo é praticar, não ser perfeito.

Can you help me?
I can’t find my hotel.
Can you speak slower, please?
Can you show me on the map?

Q1. Choose the best answer Quiz

Imagine a situação e escolha a melhor resposta.

You are lost in Paris and need help. What do you say?
A “Help me now!”
B “Excuse me, can you help me?”
C “I help you.”
Someone speaks too fast. What do you ask?
A “Speak more fast!”
B “Can you speak slower, please?”
C “You can slower?”
Someone asks: “Can you speak French?” and you cannot. What do you say?
A “No, I can’t. But I can speak English.”
B “No, I not can.”
C “I no speak French.”
You want to ask politely for someone to repeat. What do you say?
A “Repeat!”
B “You can repeat?”
C “Could you repeat that, please?”

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

You are lost in Paris. You cannot find your hotel. You see a person on the street. Ask for help: say you are lost, you cannot find your hotel, ask them to show you on the map, and ask them to speak slower.
Sugestão de resposta
“Excuse me, can you help me? I am lost. I can’t find my hotel. Can you show me on the map? Can you speak slower, please?”

Survival Card — Lesson 8: Abilities & Asking for Help

1Can you help me, please?Você pode me ajudar, por favor?
2I can’t find my hotel.Não consigo encontrar meu hotel.
3Can you speak slower, please?Pode falar mais devagar, por favor?
4Can you show me on the map?Pode me mostrar no mapa?
5I can speak English.Eu sei falar inglês.
Aula 09 — Pre-class

Where Is It? — Directions, Locations & Getting Around Paris

Depois desta aula, você consegue perguntar o caminho, dar direcções (vire à esquerda, siga reto) e dizer onde as coisas ficam em Paris.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de perguntar e dar direcções (go straight, turn left/right, cross), usar preposições de lugar (next to, across from, in front of, behind, near, far from) e perguntar “How do I get to...?” / “Where is...?” / “Is it far?”.

Vocabulário Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

Street
rua
“Walk down this street.”
Corner
esquina
“The café is on the corner.”
Turn
virar
“Turn at the corner.”
Left
esquerda
“Turn left.”
Right
direita
“Turn right at the corner.”
Straight
reto / em frente
“Go straight.”
Cross
atravessar
“Cross the street.”
Station
estação
“The metro station is near.”

V2. Match the words Matching

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a explicação com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

Street
Corner
Turn
Left
Right
Straight
Cross
Station

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context Grammar

Leia o texto com calma. As palavras em negrito são do vocabulário de hoje.

Gabriela is in Paris. She is at the metro station. She wants to find her hotel. She asks a woman: “How do I get to Hotel Le Marais?” The woman says: “Go straight on this street. Turn right at the corner. Then cross the street.” Gabriela asks: “Is it far?” The woman says: “No, it is near. The hotel is next to a café, across from the station.” Gabriela says: “Thank you! Can you show me on the map?” Now Gabriela can find her hotel!
What does Gabriela do at the corner?
A Go straight
B Turn right
C Turn left
Is the hotel far?
A Yes, it is very far.
B No, it is near.
C The text does not say.
Where is the hotel?
A Next to a café, across from the station
B Behind the metro station
C In front of the Eiffel Tower

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip Grammar

Imperatives for directions / Imperativo para direcções

DirectionGo straight.Siga reto.
DirectionTurn left / right.Vire à esquerda / direita.
DirectionCross the street.Atravesse a rua.
Ask the wayHow do I get to the hotel?Como eu chego ao hotel?
Ask locationWhere is the station?Onde fica a estação?

Prepositions of place / Preposições de lugar

next toThe hotel is next to the café.ao lado de
across fromIt is across from the station.em frente / do outro lado
in front ofThe tower is in front of the park.na frente de
behindThe park is behind the museum.atrás de
nearIt is near.perto
far fromIt is far from here.longe de
  • Direcção = verbo primeiro, sem “you”: Go straight (nunca “You go straight” ao dar ordem).
  • Sem “to”: Go straight — NUNCA “Go to straight”.
  • Sempre in front OF / across FROM / far FROM — não esqueça a preposição.

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma.

“Go .”
“Turn left at the .”
“ the street.”
“The hotel is the café.”
“How do I to the station?”

P2. Put in order Order

Coloque as frases na ordem correta para formar um pedido de direcções.

? “Turn right at the corner.”
? “Excuse me, how do I get to the hotel?”
? “Is it far?”
? “Go straight on this street.”
? “No, it is near. It is next to the café.”

S1. Read aloud Speaking

Toque em Ouvir, repita, depois grave e compare. O objetivo é praticar, não ser perfeito.

How do I get to the station?
Go straight and turn left.
Cross the street.
The hotel is next to the café.

Q1. Choose the best answer Quiz

Imagine a situação e escolha a melhor resposta.

You want to find the metro station. What do you ask?
A “Where station?”
B “Excuse me, where is the station?”
C “Station is where you?”
Someone asks the way. You want to say “forward”. What do you say?
A “Go to straight.”
B “Go straight.”
C “You straight go.”
The hotel is on the other side of the street. How do you say it?
A “It is across from the station.”
B “It is across the station.”
C “It is front the station.”
You want to know the distance. What do you ask?
A “It far is?”
B “Far it is?”
C “Is it far?”

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

You are at the metro station in Paris. You want to go to your hotel. Stop a person: ask the way, ask if it is far, and ask them to show you on the map.
Sugestão de resposta
“Excuse me, how do I get to my hotel? Is it far? Can you show me on the map?”

Survival Card — Lesson 9: Directions & Locations

1How do I get to the station?Como eu chego à estação?
2Where is the hotel?Onde fica o hotel?
3Go straight and turn left.Siga reto e vire à esquerda.
4Is it far?É longe?
5It is next to the café.Fica do lado do café.
Aula 10 — Pre-class

What Do You Look Like? — Descriptions & Character Profiles

Depois desta aula, você consegue descrever pessoas: como elas são (alta, baixa) e o que elas têm (cabelo longo, olhos azuis), usando has e is.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de descrever pessoas usando is + adjetivo (She is tall) e has + característica (She has long hair / He has blue eyes), e perguntar “What does she look like?” / “Does he have...?”.

Vocabulário Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

Tall
alto / alta
“She is tall.”
Short
baixo / baixa
“He is short.”
Hair
cabelo
“She has long hair.”
Eyes
olhos
“He has blue eyes.”
Curly
cacheado
“She has curly hair.”
Blond
loiro / loira
“She has blond hair.”
Beard
barba
“He has a beard.”
Glasses
óculos
“He has glasses.”

V2. Match the words Matching

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a explicação com calma — é aí que você aprende de verdade.

Tall
Short
Hair
Eyes
Curly
Blond
Beard
Glasses

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context Grammar

Leia o texto com calma. As palavras em negrito são do vocabulário de hoje.

This is Serena. She is tall and she is beautiful. She has long blond hair and blue eyes. Her friend is different. He is short. He has short dark hair and a beard. He has brown eyes and glasses. They are very different, but they are best friends.
What does Serena look like?
A Short, with dark hair
B Tall, with long blond hair and blue eyes
C Tall, with a beard
Does her friend have glasses?
A Yes, he has glasses.
B No, he does not.
C The text does not say.
Which word goes with “tall”?
A is (She is tall)
B has (She has tall)
C both are correct

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip Grammar

IS para a pessoa / HAS para a característica

is + adjetivoShe is tall.Ela é alta.
is + adjetivoHe is young.Ele é jovem.
has + característicaShe has long hair.Ela tem cabelo longo.
has + característicaHe has blue eyes.Ele tem olhos azuis.
Perguntar aparênciaWhat does she look like?Como ela é?
Perguntar característicaDoes he have glasses?Ele usa óculos?
  • IS com adjetivos da pessoa: tall, short, young, beautiful. NUNCA “She has tall”.
  • HAS com características: hair, eyes, a beard, glasses. NUNCA “He is blue eyes”.
  • Com hair não use “a”: She has long hair (não “a long hair”).
  • Pergunta com does: o verbo fica simples — “What does she look like?” (não “looks”).

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Leia a dica com calma.

“She tall.”
“She long hair.”
“He blue eyes.”
“He has a .”
“What does she like?”

P2. Put in order Order

Coloque as frases na ordem correta para descrever uma pessoa.

? “She has long blond hair.”
? “What does she look like?”
? “She has blue eyes.”
? “She is tall.”
? “She is beautiful.”

S1. Read aloud Speaking

Toque em Ouvir, repita, depois grave e compare. O objetivo é praticar, não ser perfeito.

What does she look like?
She has long blond hair.
He has blue eyes and a beard.
He is tall and he has short hair.

Q1. Choose the best answer Quiz

Imagine a situação e escolha a melhor resposta.

You want to say a girl is not short. What do you say?
A “She has tall.”
B “She is tall.”
C “She tall is.”
A boy’s eyes are blue. How do you say it?
A “He is blue eyes.”
B “He has blue eyes.”
C “He has a blue eyes.”
You want to ask about a person’s appearance. What do you ask?
A “What does she looks like?”
B “How is she look?”
C “What does she look like?”
You want to ask if a boy wears glasses. What do you ask?
A “Does he have glasses?”
B “Has he glasses?”
C “He have glasses?”

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

Think of your favorite character from a series. Describe the person: is she/he tall or short? What hair? What eyes? Use is and has.
Sugestão de resposta
“My favorite character is tall. She has long blond hair and blue eyes. She is beautiful.”

Survival Card — Lesson 10: Descriptions

1What does she look like?Como ela é? (aparência)
2She is tall.Ela é alta.
3She has long blond hair.Ela tem cabelo longo e loiro.
4He has blue eyes.Ele tem olhos azuis.
5He has a beard.Ele tem barba.
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

Let's Eat! -- Food, Menus & Ordering at a Restaurant

Ordering food at a cafe in Paris: restaurant vocabulary (menu, waiter, order, dish, drink, dessert) and the grammar of "would like" with countable and uncountable food words. New vocabulary: menu, waiter, order, dish, drink, dessert, delicious, bill, vegetables, fresh.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Menu -- a list of the food and drinks a restaurant has
"The waiter gives me the menu." (Cardápio)
Waiter -- the person who brings your food in a restaurant
"The waiter is very kind." (Garçom)
Order -- to ask for the food you want to eat
"I order a salad and a juice." (Pedir / fazer o pedido)
Dish -- a type of food that is ready to eat
"Pasta is my favorite dish." (Prato / prato de comida)
Drink -- something you drink, like water or juice
"What drink would you like?" (Bebida)
Dessert -- a sweet food you eat at the end of a meal
"For dessert, I would like the cake." (Sobremesa)
Delicious -- food that tastes very good
"This cake is delicious!" (Delicioso)
Bill -- the paper that shows how much your meal costs
"Can I have the bill, please?" (A conta)
Vegetables -- plants we eat, like carrots and tomatoes
"The fish comes with vegetables." (Legumes / verduras)
Fresh -- new and not old; just made or just picked
"The bread is fresh and warm." (Fresco)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Menu
Waiter
Order
Dish
Drink
Dessert
Delicious
Bill
Vegetables
Fresh

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela is at a small cafe in Paris. The waiter brings the menu and asks what she would like. She reads the dishes and looks at the food. There are fresh vegetables, warm bread, and some cheese. Gabriela says, "I would like the grilled chicken, please, and an orange juice." The waiter writes her order. The chicken is fresh and the food is delicious. For dessert, she would like a slice of chocolate cake. At the end of the meal, she asks for the bill and says thank you. She feels proud, because now she can order a whole meal in English.

1. Who brings the menu to Gabriela?
A The waiter brings the menu.
B Her friend brings the menu.
C Nobody brings the menu.
2. What does Gabriela order for her main dish?
A She orders pasta with tomato sauce.
B She orders the grilled chicken and an orange juice.
C She orders only a dessert.
3. What does Gabriela do at the end of the meal?
A She leaves without paying.
B She asks for the bill and says thank you.
C She orders a second menu.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- would like + countable / uncountable

GRAMMAR

Usamos "would like" para pedir comida de forma educada, e "a / an / some / any" com palavras contáveis e incontáveis. / We use "would like" to order food politely, and a / an / some / any with countable and uncountable words.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Polite requestI would like + nounI would like a salad.
Countable (one)a / an + singularI would like a juice.
Uncountable / plural (+)some + nounI would like some water.
QuestionWould you like...? / anyWould you like any dessert?

Key point / Ponto-chave: use would like + comida para pedir de forma educada. Use a / an com palavras contáveis no singular (a juice, an apple) e some com incontáveis ou plurais (some water, some vegetables). Em perguntas e frases negativas, use any (Would you like any cake?).

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"The brings the menu."
"I would like to the chicken."
"For , I would like the cake."
"This pasta is ."
"Can I have the , please?"
"The bread is warm and ."

Stage 2: Put the Restaurant Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de uma refeição na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, you say what you would like to order.
?First, the waiter brings the menu.
?Finally, you ask for the bill and pay.
?Next, you choose your food and drink.
?Then, the waiter brings your meal.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

I would like to see the menu, please.
Eu gostaria de ver o cardápio, por favor.
I would like the chicken and some vegetables.
Eu gostaria do frango e de alguns legumes.
The cake is delicious. Thank you.
O bolo está delicioso. Obrigada.
Can I have the bill, please?
Pode me trazer a conta, por favor?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to order a drink politely. You say:
A "I would like an orange juice, please."
B "Give me juice now."
C "I want juice you bring."
You want some water (uncountable). You say:
A "I would like a water."
B "I would like some water."
C "I would like many water."
You finished your meal and want to pay. You say:
A "Where is my menu again?"
B "Can I have the bill, please?"
C "I would like to start, please."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine you are at a cafe in Paris. Order a full meal out loud: a drink, a main dish with some vegetables, and a dessert. Then ask for the bill. Use "I would like" and at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 11

1I would like to see the menu, please.Eu gostaria de ver o cardápio, por favor.
2I would like the chicken and some vegetables.Eu gostaria do frango e de alguns legumes.
3What would you like to drink?O que você gostaria de beber?
4For dessert, I would like the chocolate cake.De sobremesa, eu gostaria do bolo de chocolate.
5Can I have the bill, please?Pode me trazer a conta, por favor?
Aula 12 -- Pre-class

Shopping in the City -- Clothes, Prices & Choices

Shopping for clothes at a boutique in Paris: shop vocabulary (clothes, size, price, sale, fitting room) and the grammar of "How much" and comparative adjectives (cheaper, bigger, more expensive). New vocabulary: clothes, size, price, cheap, expensive, try on, fitting room, sale, change.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Clothes -- the things people wear, like shirts and jackets
"I want to buy new clothes for the winter." (Roupas)
Size -- how big or small a piece of clothing is
"What size is this shirt?" (Tamanho)
Price -- how much money something costs
"The price of the jacket is high." (Preço)
Cheap -- it does not cost much money; low price
"This white shirt is very cheap." (Barato)
Expensive -- it costs a lot of money; high price
"The blue jacket is expensive." (Caro)
Try on -- to put clothes on to see if they fit you
"Can I try on this jacket?" (Experimentar / provar)
Fitting room -- a small room in a store where you try on clothes
"The fitting room is near the stairs." (Provador)
Sale -- a time when a store sells things for a lower price
"The shirts are on sale this week." (Promoção / liquidação)
Change -- the money the store gives back to you after you pay
"Here is your change, thank you." (Troco)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Clothes
Size
Price
Cheap
Expensive
Try on
Fitting room
Sale
Change

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela goes shopping at a boutique in Paris. She looks at the clothes and finds two jackets. The blue jacket is nice, but the price is high: it is more expensive than the gray one. The gray jacket is cheaper, and it is also on sale this week. Gabriela asks the shop assistant, "How much is the gray jacket?" Then she goes to the fitting room to try on the jacket. It is a little big, so she asks for a smaller size. The medium size is perfect. She buys the gray jacket and a white shirt, pays, and the assistant gives her the change. She is happy, because she did all her shopping in English.

1. Which jacket is more expensive?
A The blue jacket is more expensive.
B The gray jacket is more expensive.
C Both jackets cost the same.
2. Why does Gabriela go to the fitting room?
A To pay for the jacket.
B To try on the jacket.
C To ask for the menu.
3. What does the shop assistant give her at the end?
A A bigger jacket.
B Her change.
C A free dessert.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- How much & comparative adjectives

GRAMMAR

Usamos "How much" para perguntar o preço, e comparativos para comparar duas coisas. / We use "How much" to ask the price, and comparatives to compare two things.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Ask the priceHow much + is/areHow much is this jacket?
Short adjectiveadjective + er + thancheap → cheaper than
Short + double letterbig → biggerbig → bigger than
Long adjectivemore + adjective + thanmore expensive than
Irregulargood → bettergood → better than

Key point / Ponto-chave: use How much para perguntar o preço (How much is it?). Para comparar duas coisas, adjetivos curtos ganham -er (cheap → cheaper, big → bigger) e adjetivos longos usam more (more expensive). Sempre use than depois do comparativo.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"The white shirt is than the blue one."
"The blue jacket is more than the gray one."
" is this top?"
"Can I this jacket?"
"The shirts are on this week."
"Here is your , thank you."

Stage 2: Put the Shopping Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de uma compra na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, you try the clothes on in the fitting room.
?First, you look for the clothes you want.
?Finally, you pay and the assistant gives you your change.
?Next, you check the price and the size.
?Then, you choose the one you like more.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

How much is this jacket?
Quanto custa esta jaqueta?
The gray one is cheaper than the blue one.
A cinza é mais barata que a azul.
Can I try on this one, please?
Posso experimentar esta, por favor?
Do you have a smaller size?
Você tem um tamanho menor?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to know the price of a jacket. You say:
A "How much is this jacket?"
B "How many is this jacket?"
C "How old is this jacket?"
The white shirt costs less than the black top. You say:
A "The white shirt is more cheap than the top."
B "The white shirt is cheaper than the top."
C "The white shirt is cheap than the top."
You want to test a jacket in the store. You say:
A "Can I order this jacket?"
B "Can I try on this jacket?"
C "Can I pay this jacket?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine you are at a boutique in Paris. Describe two jackets out loud: ask the price, compare them with cheaper and more expensive, say which one you try on, and choose one. Use "How much" and at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 12

1How much is this jacket?Quanto custa esta jaqueta?
2Can I try on this one, please?Posso experimentar esta, por favor?
3The gray one is cheaper than the blue one.A cinza é mais barata que a azul.
4Do you have a smaller size?Você tem um tamanho menor?
5I will take this one. Here is the money.Vou levar esta. Aqui está o dinheiro.
Aula 13 -- Pre-class

Getting Around the City -- Transport & Directions

Getting around Paris by subway and bus and asking people for directions: city vocabulary (subway, station, map, ticket, corner) and the grammar of asking the way ("Where is", "How do I get to") and giving directions with the imperative (Go straight, Turn left, Get off). New vocabulary: subway, bus stop, station, map, ticket, get off, corner, go straight, across from.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Subway -- an underground train in a big city
"I take the subway to the city center." (Metrô)
Bus stop -- the place where you wait to get on a bus
"The bus stop is across from the cafe." (Ponto de ônibus)
Station -- a place where trains or the subway stop to take people
"Get off at the next station." (Estação)
Map -- a picture that shows the streets and places of a city
"Look at the map to find the museum." (Mapa)
Ticket -- a small paper you buy to take a bus, train or subway
"I bought a ticket for the bus." (Bilhete / passagem)
Get off -- to leave a bus, train or subway at a stop
"Get off at the third stop." (Descer / saltar)
Corner -- the place where two streets meet
"Turn right at the corner." (Esquina)
Go straight -- to keep going forward, without turning
"Go straight for two minutes." (Siga em frente / reto)
Across from -- on the other side of the street, facing a place
"The cafe is across from the station." (Em frente a / do outro lado)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Subway
Bus stop
Station
Map
Ticket
Get off
Corner
Go straight
Across from

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela wants to visit a museum in Paris. She takes the subway to the city center and buys a ticket. She asks a local, "How do I get to the museum?" The local says, "Get off at the next station. Then go straight and turn left at the corner. The museum is on your left, next to a cafe." Gabriela looks at the map and follows the directions. The bus stop for the Eiffel Tower is across from the museum. She does not get lost, and she is happy, because she found her way in English.

1. How does Gabriela get to the city center?
A She takes the subway.
B She takes a plane.
C She drives a car.
2. Where does the local tell her to turn?
A Turn right at the corner.
B Turn left at the corner.
C Go back to the station.
3. Where is the bus stop for the Eiffel Tower?
A Inside the museum.
B Across from the museum.
C At the airport.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Asking the way & giving directions

GRAMMAR

Usamos "Where is" e "How do I get to" para perguntar o caminho, e o imperativo para dar direções. / We use "Where is" and "How do I get to" to ask the way, and the imperative to give directions.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Ask whereWhere is + placeWhere is the museum?
Ask the wayHow do I get to + placeHow do I get to the station?
Give a directionverb only (imperative)Go straight. Turn left.
Tell where to leaveGet off + at + placeGet off at the next stop.
Say which sideon your left / across fromIt is across from the cafe.

Key point / Ponto-chave: use Where is ou How do I get to para perguntar o caminho. Para dar uma direção, use só o verbo (o imperativo): Go straight, Turn left, Turn right, Get off. Não use "I" nem "you" antes do verbo.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

" the station?"
" for two minutes."
"Turn left at the ."
" at the next station."
"I bought a for the subway."
"The cafe is the station."

Stage 2: Put the Trip Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de uma viagem pela cidade na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, you get off at the right station.
?First, you look at the map to find the place.
?Finally, you go straight and turn left or right at the corner.
?Next, you take the subway or the bus to the city center.
?Then, you ask a local how to get to the place.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

Excuse me, where is the station?
Com licença, onde fica a estação?
How do I get to the museum?
Como eu chego ao museu?
Go straight and turn left at the corner.
Siga em frente e vire à esquerda na esquina.
Get off at the next station.
Desça na próxima estação.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You are lost and want to find the museum. You ask:
A "How do I get to the museum?"
B "How much is the museum?"
C "How old is the museum?"
You tell a tourist to keep going forward. You say:
A "You go straight."
B "Go straight."
C "Going straight."
You want to leave the subway at the next station. You say:
A "Get down at the next station."
B "I get off at the next station."
C "I get in at the next station."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine a friend is lost in Paris and wants to reach the museum. Out loud, give the directions: tell them to take the subway, get off at the right station, go straight, and turn left at the corner. Use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 13

1Excuse me, where is the station?Com licença, onde fica a estação?
2How do I get to the museum?Como eu chego ao museu?
3Go straight and turn left at the corner.Siga em frente e vire à esquerda na esquina.
4Get off at the next station.Desça na próxima estação.
5Is it far? Can I walk there?É longe? Posso ir a pé?
Aula 14 -- Pre-class

Eating Out Like a Local -- Ordering & Food

Ordering a whole dinner at a restaurant in Paris: restaurant vocabulary (reservation, appetizer, main course, the check, tip) and the polite language to order ("I would like", "Could I have", "Can you recommend"). New vocabulary: reservation, table for two, appetizer, main course, spicy, well-done, recommend, the check, tip.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Reservation -- a table you book before you arrive at a restaurant
"We have a reservation for tonight." (Reserva)
Table for two -- a table at a restaurant for two people
"A table for two, please." (Mesa para dois)
Appetizer -- a small dish you eat before the main course
"For an appetizer, I would like soup." (Entrada)
Main course -- the biggest and most important dish of the meal
"For the main course, I would like chicken." (Prato principal)
Spicy -- food that tastes hot in your mouth, like chili or pepper
"This fish is too spicy for me." (Apimentado)
Well-done -- meat that is cooked a lot, not pink inside
"I would like my steak well-done." (Bem passado)
Recommend -- to tell someone that a dish is good and they should try it
"Can you recommend a good dish?" (Recomendar)
The check -- the paper that shows how much you pay at the end of a meal
"Could I have the check, please?" (A conta)
Tip -- extra money you leave for good service at a restaurant
"I left a small tip for the waiter." (Gorjeta)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Reservation
Table for two
Appetizer
Main course
Spicy
Well-done
Recommend
The check
Tip

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela goes to a restaurant in Paris with her family. They have a reservation for a table for two. The waiter brings the menu and Gabriela asks, "Can you recommend a good dish?" The waiter recommends the spicy fish, but Gabriela does not like spicy food. She says, "I would like the chicken, please. And a soup for an appetizer." For her mom, she orders a steak, well-done, and for the main course everyone is happy. At the end, Gabriela asks, "Could I have the check, please?" She leaves a small tip for the waiter, because the service was very good.

1. How does Gabriela ask the waiter for advice?
A "Can you recommend a good dish?"
B "How much is the dish?"
C "Where is the dish?"
2. Why does Gabriela not order the fish?
A Because it is too expensive.
B Because it is too spicy for her.
C Because there is no fish.
3. What does she do at the end?
A She leaves without paying.
B She asks for the check and leaves a tip.
C She orders another dinner.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Ordering politely

GRAMMAR

Usamos "I would like", "Could I have" e "Can you recommend" para pedir com educação em um restaurante. / We use "I would like", "Could I have" and "Can you recommend" to order politely.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Order foodI would like + foodI would like the chicken.
Short form (speaking)I'd like + foodI'd like a soup, please.
Ask for somethingCould I have + thing?Could I have the check?
Ask for adviceCan you recommend + ...?Can you recommend a dish?
Say how you want itnoun + well-doneMy steak, well-done.

Key point / Ponto-chave: para pedir comida com educação, use I would like (forma curta: I'd like), não "I want". Para pedir algo, use Could I have. Para pedir uma sugestão, use Can you recommend. Termine sempre com please.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

" the chicken, please."
" the check, please?"
"Can you a good dish?"
"For an , I would like soup."
"We have a for tonight."
"I would like my steak ."

Stage 2: Put the Dinner Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de um jantar no restaurante na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, you order your appetizer and your main course.
?First, you tell the host you have a reservation.
?Finally, you ask for the check and you leave a small tip.
?Next, the waiter brings the menu and you ask, can you recommend something?
?Then, you eat your meal and you order a dessert.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

We have a reservation for a table for two.
Temos uma reserva para uma mesa para dois.
Can you recommend a good dish?
Você pode recomendar um prato bom?
I would like the chicken, please.
Eu gostaria do frango, por favor.
Could I have the check, please?
Você pode trazer a conta, por favor?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to order the chicken politely. You say:
A "I would like the chicken, please."
B "I want the chicken."
C "Give me the chicken."
You finished dinner and want to pay. You say:
A "Where is the check?"
B "Could I have the check, please?"
C "I pay now."
You do not know what to order. You ask the waiter:
A "How old is the dish?"
B "Can you recommend a good dish?"
C "Is the dish here?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine you are at a restaurant in Paris. Out loud, order a whole dinner: tell the waiter you have a reservation, ask him to recommend a dish, order an appetizer and a main course politely, say how you want it, and ask for the check. Use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 14

1We have a reservation for a table for two.Temos uma reserva para uma mesa para dois.
2Can you recommend a good dish?Você pode recomendar um prato bom?
3I would like the chicken, please.Eu gostaria do frango, por favor.
4My steak, well-done, please.Meu bife, bem passado, por favor.
5Could I have the check, please?Você pode trazer a conta, por favor?
Aula 15 -- Pre-class

A Day at the Museum -- Tickets & Art

Visiting a museum in Paris: museum vocabulary (entrance, exhibition, gallery, painting, sculpture, masterpiece) and the language to buy a ticket and describe what you see ("How much is / How much are", "There is / There are"). New vocabulary: museum, entrance, exhibition, gallery, painting, sculpture, artist, guide, masterpiece.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Museum -- a building where you go to see art and history
"The museum in Paris is very famous." (Museu)
Entrance -- the door or place where you go into a building
"The entrance is on your right." (Entrada)
Exhibition -- a group of works of art shown for people to see
"There is a beautiful exhibition of French art." (Exposição)
Gallery -- a large room in a museum where art is shown
"There are many paintings in this gallery." (Galeria)
Painting -- a picture an artist makes with colors and a brush
"This painting is very old and famous." (Pintura / Quadro)
Sculpture -- a work of art in three dimensions, often in stone or metal
"There are many sculptures on the second floor." (Escultura)
Artist -- a person who makes art, such as paintings or sculptures
"This artist is very famous in France." (Artista)
Guide -- a person who walks with you and explains the art
"A guide explained each room to us." (Guia)
Masterpiece -- a very special and important work of art by a great artist
"This painting is a real masterpiece." (Obra-prima)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Museum
Entrance
Exhibition
Gallery
Painting
Sculpture
Artist
Guide
Masterpiece

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela visits a famous museum in Paris. At the entrance, she asks, "How much is a student ticket?" It is only six euros, so she shows her student card. Inside, there is a beautiful exhibition of French art. On the first floor, there is a famous painting; it is a real masterpiece. On the second floor, there are many sculptures by a great artist. A guide walks with Gabriela and explains each gallery. She takes many photos, but she does not use the flash.

1. How does Gabriela ask the price of a ticket?
A "How much is a student ticket?"
B "Where is a student ticket?"
C "Who is a student ticket?"
2. What is on the first floor?
A Many sculptures.
B A famous painting, a real masterpiece.
C The entrance.
3. Who explains the galleries to Gabriela?
A The artist.
B A guide.
C Her mom.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Prices & There is / There are

GRAMMAR

Usamos "How much is/are" para perguntar preços e "There is/are" para dizer o que existe. Use "is" para uma coisa e "are" para várias. / We use "How much is/are" to ask prices and "There is/are" to say what exists.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Ask price (one)How much is + singular?How much is a ticket?
Ask price (many)How much are + plural?How much are two tickets?
One thing existsThere is + singularThere is a painting.
Many things existThere are + pluralThere are many sculptures.
Describe itIt is / They are + adjectiveIt is beautiful.

Key point / Ponto-chave: use is para uma coisa e are para várias. Para perguntar preço: How much is (uma) / How much are (várias). Para dizer o que existe: There is (uma) / There are (várias).

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"How much a student ticket?"
"How much two tickets?"
"There a famous painting here."
"There many sculptures in this gallery."
"The is on your right."
"This painting is a real ."

Stage 2: Put the Museum Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de uma visita ao museu na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, you walk into the first gallery and you see the exhibition.
?First, you go to the entrance and you ask, how much is a ticket?
?Finally, you take some photos, but you do not use the flash.
?Next, you buy your ticket and you show your student card.
?Then, you look at the paintings and the sculptures, and a guide explains them.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

How much is a student ticket?
Quanto custa um ingresso de estudante?
There is a famous painting here.
Aqui há uma pintura famosa.
There are many sculptures in this gallery.
Há muitas esculturas nesta galeria.
This painting is a real masterpiece.
Esta pintura é uma verdadeira obra-prima.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to know the price of one ticket. You ask:
A "How much is a ticket?"
B "How much are a ticket?"
C "How many is a ticket?"
You see many sculptures in the gallery. You say:
A "There is many sculptures."
B "There are many sculptures."
C "There be many sculptures."
You see one famous painting. You say:
A "There are a famous painting."
B "There is a famous painting."
C "There has a famous painting."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine you are at a museum in Paris. Out loud, describe your visit: ask how much a student ticket is, find the entrance, and describe what is in the exhibition. Use "There is" for one thing and "There are" for many. Use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 15

1How much is a student ticket?Quanto custa um ingresso de estudante?
2Where is the entrance?Onde fica a entrada?
3There is a famous painting here.Aqui há uma pintura famosa.
4There are many sculptures in this gallery.Há muitas esculturas nesta galeria.
5This painting is a real masterpiece.Esta pintura é uma verdadeira obra-prima.
Aula 16 -- Pre-class

Asking About the City -- A Guided Tour & Getting Information

A guided tour of Paris: tour vocabulary (tour, sightseeing, landmark, river cruise, brochure) and the language to ask for information politely ("Can you tell me...?", "Is there...?"). New vocabulary: tour, sightseeing, landmark, river cruise, view, brochure, opening hours, souvenir, nearby.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Tour -- a visit to many interesting places, often with a guide
"The city tour starts at ten o'clock." (Passeio / Tour)
Sightseeing -- the activity of visiting famous and interesting places
"We went sightseeing all morning in Paris." (Passear pelos pontos turísticos)
Landmark -- a famous building or place that is easy to recognize
"The Eiffel Tower is the most famous landmark in Paris." (Ponto de referência)
River cruise -- a trip on a boat along a river to see the city
"The river cruise gives you a great view of Paris." (Passeio de barco no rio)
View -- everything you can see from a place, often beautiful
"From the boat, the view of the city is wonderful." (Vista)
Brochure -- a small paper book with pictures and information about a place
"Take a brochure; it has a map of the tour." (Folheto)
Opening hours -- the times when a place is open for visitors
"The opening hours are from nine to six." (Horário de funcionamento)
Souvenir -- a small thing you buy to remember a place or trip
"I bought a souvenir for my mom at the shop." (Lembrança / Souvenir)
Nearby -- close to here, only a short distance away
"There is an old church nearby." (Por perto / Próximo)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Tour
Sightseeing
Landmark
River cruise
View
Brochure
Opening hours
Souvenir
Nearby

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela goes on a guided tour in Paris. At the start, she asks, "Can you tell me where the tour starts?" The guide shows her the first landmark. Then the group takes a river cruise, and the view from the boat is wonderful. Gabriela asks, "Is there a place to take photos?" The guide says yes. After the cruise, they go sightseeing and visit an old church nearby. She asks about the opening hours, and they are from nine to six. At the end, she takes a brochure with a map and buys a small souvenir for her mom.

1. How does Gabriela ask politely where the tour starts?
A "Can you tell me where the tour starts?"
B "Where starts the tour you?"
C "How much is the tour?"
2. What gives a wonderful view of the city?
A The brochure.
B The river cruise from the boat.
C The souvenir shop.
3. What are the opening hours of the church?
A From ten to two.
B From nine to six.
C All night.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Can you tell me...? & Is there...?

GRAMMAR

Usamos "Can you tell me...?" para pedir informação de forma educada e "Is there...?" para perguntar se algo existe. Depois de "Can you tell me", mantemos a ordem normal (sujeito + verbo). / We use "Can you tell me...?" to ask for information politely and "Is there...?" to ask if something exists.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Ask politely (place)Can you tell me where + subject + verb?Can you tell me where the tour starts?
Ask politely (time)Can you tell me what time + subject + verb?Can you tell me what time it opens?
Ask politely (length)Can you tell me how long + subject + verb?Can you tell me how long the tour is?
Ask if it existsIs there + a/an + noun?Is there a shop nearby?
Short answerYes, there is. / No, there isn't.Yes, there is.

Key point / Ponto-chave: depois de Can you tell me, mantenha a ordem normal (sujeito + verbo): "Can you tell me where the tour starts?" (não "where does the tour start"). Para perguntar se algo existe, use Is there + a/an + substantivo.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"Can you tell me the tour starts?"
"Can you tell me it opens?"
" a souvenir shop nearby?"
"The Eiffel Tower is a famous ."
"Take a ; it has a map."
"I bought a for my mom."

Stage 2: Put the Tour Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de um passeio guiado na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, you take a river cruise and you enjoy the view of the city.
?First, you meet the guide and you ask, can you tell me where the tour starts?
?Finally, you buy a souvenir and you take a brochure with a map.
?Next, you walk to the first landmark and you listen to the guide.
?Then, you visit an old church nearby and you ask about the opening hours.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

Can you tell me where the tour starts?
Você pode me dizer onde o passeio começa?
Is there a souvenir shop nearby?
Tem uma loja de lembranças por perto?
Can you tell me how long the tour is?
Você pode me dizer quanto tempo dura o passeio?
The view from the river cruise is wonderful.
A vista do passeio de barco é maravilhosa.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to know politely where the tour starts. You ask:
A "Can you tell me where the tour starts?"
B "Can you tell me where does the tour start?"
C "Where the tour?"
You want to know if there is a shop nearby. You ask:
A "There is a shop nearby?"
B "Is there a shop nearby?"
C "Is nearby a shop there?"
You want to know what time the cruise leaves. You ask:
A "Can you tell me what time leaves the cruise?"
B "Can you tell me what time the cruise leaves?"
C "What time the cruise?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine you are on a guided tour in Paris. Out loud, ask the guide for information: where the tour starts, what time the cruise leaves, how long it is, and if there is a souvenir shop nearby. Use "Can you tell me..." and "Is there..." Use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 16

1Can you tell me where the tour starts?Você pode me dizer onde o passeio começa?
2Can you tell me what time it opens?Você pode me dizer a que horas abre?
3Is there a souvenir shop nearby?Tem uma loja de lembranças por perto?
4Can you tell me how long the tour is?Você pode me dizer quanto tempo dura o passeio?
5The view from the river cruise is wonderful.A vista do passeio de barco é maravilhosa.
Aula 17 -- Pre-class

Buying Gifts & Making Choices

Shopping for gifts in a Paris gift shop: gift vocabulary (gift, present, postcard, keychain, jewelry) and the language to choose between things ("Which one...?", "I'll take the blue one", "I prefer the small ones"). New vocabulary: gift, gift shop, present, postcard, keychain, magnet, jewelry, wallet, wrapping paper.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Gift -- a thing you give to someone, often for a special day
"I bought a gift for my best friend." (Presente)
Gift shop -- a store where you buy presents and souvenirs
"There is a gift shop at the end of the tour." (Loja de presentes)
Present -- another word for a gift: a thing you give to someone
"This scarf is a present for my mom." (Presente)
Postcard -- a small card with a picture that you send by mail
"I sent a postcard of the Eiffel Tower to my friend." (Cartão-postal)
Keychain -- a small ring or chain that you put your keys on
"The blue keychain is very popular." (Chaveiro)
Magnet -- a small object that sticks to metal, often on a fridge
"I bought a fridge magnet for my dad." (Ímã / ímã de geladeira)
Jewelry -- pretty things you wear, like rings, necklaces and earrings
"The silver jewelry is perfect for a present." (Jóias / bijuterias)
Wallet -- a small flat case where you keep your money and cards
"I bought a leather wallet for my brother." (Carteira)
Wrapping paper -- colorful paper you use to cover a present
"The clerk wrapped my gift in nice wrapping paper." (Papel de presente)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Gift
Gift shop
Present
Postcard
Keychain
Magnet
Jewelry
Wallet
Wrapping paper

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela goes to a gift shop in Paris to buy a present for everyone. The clerk asks, "Which one would you like?" Gabriela looks at the postcards. There are big ones and small ones, and she takes three small ones for her friends. For the keychain, she says, "I'll take the blue one." For her mom, the silver jewelry is perfect. She also buys a magnet for her dad and a wallet for her brother. The clerk covers each gift in nice wrapping paper.

1. How does the clerk ask Gabriela to choose?
A "Which one would you like?"
B "How much is the tour?"
C "Where are you from?"
2. Which postcards does she take?
A The big ones.
B Three small ones.
C None of them.
3. What does she choose for her mom?
A A blue keychain.
B The silver jewelry.
C A leather wallet.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- one / ones & Which one

GRAMMAR

Para não repetir o substantivo, usamos "one" para uma coisa e "ones" para várias. Perguntamos "Which one...?" e escolhemos com "I'll take...". / To avoid repeating the noun, we use "one" for one thing and "ones" for many.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Ask which (choose)Which one + question?Which one would you like?
One thing (singular)the + adjective + onethe blue one
Many things (plural)the + adjective + onesthe small ones
Buy itI'll take + the one/onesI'll take the silver ones.
PreferI prefer + the one/onesI prefer the small ones.

Key point / Ponto-chave: use one para uma coisa e ones para várias, sem repetir o substantivo: "the blue one" (não "the blue keychain one"). Pergunte com Which one e compre com I'll take.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"I'll take the blue , please."
"I prefer the small ."
"Which would you like?"
"This scarf is a for my mom."
"The silver is perfect for a present."
"Can you put it in ?"

Stage 2: Put the Gift Shop Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de comprar um presente na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, the clerk asks, which one would you like?
?First, you walk into the gift shop and you look at the gifts.
?Finally, the clerk wraps your present in wrapping paper and you pay.
?Next, you find the postcards and you choose the small ones.
?Then, you say, I'll take the blue one, please.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

I'll take the blue one, please.
Vou levar o azul, por favor.
I prefer the small ones.
Eu prefiro os pequenos.
Which one do you recommend?
Qual você recomenda?
Can you put it in wrapping paper?
Você pode embrulhar para presente?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want one blue keychain. You say:
A "I'll take the blue one."
B "I'll take the blue ones."
C "I'll take the blue one keychain."
You prefer the small postcards (many). You say:
A "I prefer the small one."
B "I prefer the small ones."
C "I prefer the small one postcards."
You want the clerk to help you choose one magnet. You ask:
A "Which ones do you recommend?"
B "Which one do you recommend?"
C "Which magnet one?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine you are in a gift shop in Paris. Out loud, choose presents for three people: answer "Which one would you like?", say "I'll take the blue one" and "I prefer the small ones", and ask for wrapping paper. Use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 17

1I'll take the blue one, please.Vou levar o azul, por favor.
2I prefer the small ones.Eu prefiro os pequenos.
3Which one do you recommend?Qual você recomenda?
4This is a present for my mom.Este é um presente para a minha mãe.
5Can you put it in wrapping paper?Você pode embrulhar para presente?
Aula 18 -- Pre-class

Sending a Postcard

At the post office in Paris: mail vocabulary (stamp, envelope, mailbox, address, parcel) and the short words to say who you send things to ("send it to her", "send them to him"). New vocabulary: post office, stamp, envelope, address, mailbox, abroad, by air mail, parcel, weigh.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Post office -- a place where you send letters, postcards and parcels
"I went to the post office to send my postcards." (Correio / agência dos correios)
Stamp -- a small paper you stick on mail to pay for sending it
"The stamp for Brazil is one euro eighty." (Selo)
Envelope -- a flat paper cover that you put a letter inside
"I put the letter in an envelope and wrote the address." (Envelope)
Address -- the place where someone lives, that you write on the mail
"I copied my grandmother's address on the postcard." (Endereço)
Mailbox -- a box on the street where you put letters to send them
"I put the postcard in the yellow mailbox." (Caixa de correio)
Abroad -- in or to another country, far from home
"To send a postcard abroad, you need a bigger stamp." (Para o exterior)
By air mail -- when a letter or postcard travels to another country by plane
"I sent it to Brazil by air mail." (Por via aérea)
Parcel -- a box or package that you send to someone by mail
"The clerk weighed a small parcel for my mom." (Encomenda / pacote)
Weigh -- to measure how heavy something is, often on a scale
"The clerk will weigh your parcel to find the price." (Pesar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Post office
Stamp
Envelope
Address
Mailbox
Abroad
By air mail
Parcel
Weigh

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela goes to the post office in Paris. She has a postcard for her mom, and she wants to send it to her in Brazil. The clerk says, "That is abroad, so you need a stamp for one euro eighty, and we send it by air mail." Gabriela also has two postcards for her friends, so she sends them too. She wrote the address on each one. For her dad, she has a small parcel; the clerk will weigh it and send it to him. Then Gabriela puts everything in the yellow mailbox.

1. Where does Gabriela want to send the postcard for her mom?
A To her in Brazil.
B To the museum.
C To the gift shop.
2. What does she do with the two postcards for her friends?
A She keeps them.
B She sends them too.
C She gives them to the clerk for free.
3. What does the clerk do with the parcel for her dad?
A He opens it.
B He weighs it and sends it to him.
C He throws it away.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- it / them / her / him

GRAMMAR

Para não repetir o nome, usamos uma palavra curta depois do verbo: "it" para uma coisa, "them" para várias, "her" para uma mulher, "him" para um homem. / To avoid repeating the name, we use a short word after the verb.

Instead of / Em vez deWe say / DizemosExample / Exemplo
the postcard (uma coisa)itI'll send it.
two postcards (várias coisas)themI'll send them.
my mom (mulher)hersend it to her.
my dad (homem)himsend it to him.
my friends (pessoas)themwrite to them.

Key point / Ponto-chave: depois do verbo, use a palavra curta para não repetir o nome: it (uma coisa), them (várias coisas ou pessoas), her (mulher), him (homem). Estrutura: send + it/them + to + her/him/them.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"I have a postcard. I'll send to my friend."
"I have two postcards. I'll send to Brazil."
"This is for my mom. I'll send it to ."
"This is for my dad. I'll send it to ."
"I put a on the postcard."
"I sent it abroad ."

Stage 2: Put the Postcard Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de enviar um postal na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, you go to the post office and you choose a stamp.
?First, you buy a postcard and you write a short message on the back.
?Finally, you put it in the yellow mailbox and you send it to them.
?Next, you copy the address of your family carefully.
?Then, you give the postcard to the clerk and you pay.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

I want to send it to her in Brazil.
Quero enviar isto para ela no Brasil.
Can I send them too?
Posso enviar estes também?
How much is a stamp for Brazil?
Quanto custa um selo para o Brasil?
Can you weigh this parcel?
Você pode pesar esta encomenda?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You have one postcard for your mom. You say:
A "I'll send it to her."
B "I'll send them to her."
C "I'll send it to him."
You have three postcards for your friends. You say:
A "I'll send it to them."
B "I'll send them to my friends."
C "I'll send them to her."
You want to send a postcard to Brazil. You ask:
A "Where is the museum?"
B "How much is a stamp to send it abroad?"
C "Which one would you like?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine you are at the post office in Paris. Out loud, send a postcard to your mom, two postcards to your friends, and a parcel to your dad. Use "it", "them", "her" and "him", and use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 18

1I want to send it to her in Brazil.Quero enviar isto para ela no Brasil.
2Can I send them too?Posso enviar estes também?
3How much is a stamp for Brazil?Quanto custa um selo para o Brasil?
4Can you weigh this parcel?Você pode pesar esta encomenda?
5Where is the mailbox?Onde fica a caixa de correio?
Aula 19 -- Pre-class

Making Plans with Friends

Arranging to meet up with friends in Paris: plan vocabulary (plan, meet up, get together, hang out, free, busy) and the language to talk about future plans ("I'm meeting Lucas at four", "We're going to the movies") and to suggest ("Let's meet up", "Why don't we get together?"). New vocabulary: plan, meet up, get together, hang out, free, busy, on time, downtown, looking forward to.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Plan -- an idea about what you will do, where and when
"My plan for Saturday is to see my friends." (Plano)
Meet up -- to see your friends at a place you both choose
"Let's meet up at the cafe at four." (Encontrar-se)
Get together -- when a group of friends meet to spend time together
"We get together every Saturday." (Reunir-se / juntar-se)
Hang out -- to spend relaxed, easy time with friends
"We're going to hang out at the park." (Passar o tempo / curtir)
Free -- available, with nothing you must do at that time
"Are you free on Saturday afternoon?" (Livre / disponível)
Busy -- with a lot of things to do, so not available
"I'm busy in the morning with my class." (Ocupado/a)
On time -- at the right time, not early and not late
"Please be on time; the movie starts at six." (Na hora / pontual)
Downtown -- the central, busy part of a city, with shops and cafes
"We're meeting downtown near the park." (Centro da cidade)
Looking forward to -- feeling happy and excited about something that will happen
"I'm looking forward to the weekend." (Ansioso/a por / mal posso esperar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Plan
Meet up
Get together
Hang out
Free
Busy
On time
Downtown
Looking forward to

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela has a plan for Saturday. Her friend Lucas calls and says, "We're getting together this weekend. Are you free?" In the morning she's busy with her French class, but in the afternoon she's free. Lucas says, "Let's meet up at the cafe downtown at four. Then we're going to the movies at six." Gabriela wants to be on time, so she writes the time down. "Why don't we hang out at the cafe first?" Lucas asks. "Perfect," says Gabriela. "I'm really looking forward to it!"

1. When is Gabriela free?
A In the morning.
B In the afternoon.
C She is busy all day.
2. Where are they meeting up?
A At the cafe downtown.
B At the post office.
C At her French class.
3. What are they doing after the cafe?
A Going home.
B Going to the movies at six.
C Going to the French class.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Continuous for the Future & Let's / Why don't we

GRAMMAR

Para um plano futuro já combinado, usamos be (am/is/are) + verbo-ing, geralmente com um horário ou dia. Para sugerir, usamos "Let's + verbo" ou "Why don't we + verbo?". / For a future plan you arranged, use be + verb-ing. To suggest, use Let's or Why don't we.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Plan (I)I am + verb-ingI'm meeting Lucas at four.
Plan (we / they)We are + verb-ingWe're going to the movies.
Plan (he / she)He/She is + verb-ingLucas is coming too.
Suggest (idea)Let's + simple verbLet's meet up at the cafe.
Suggest (question)Why don't we + verb?Why don't we get together?

Key point / Ponto-chave: para um plano futuro use be + verbo-ing ("I'm meeting", não "I meet"). Depois de Let's, use o verbo simples ("Let's go", não "Let's to go").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"I'm Lucas at four o'clock."
" meet up at the cafe."
"Are you on Saturday?"
"In the morning I'm with my class."
"Please be ; the movie starts at six."
"I'm really it."

Stage 2: Put the Plan Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de combinar um plano na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, your friend asks, where are we getting together?
?First, you send a message to your friend.
?Finally, you confirm the plan and you say, see you on Saturday.
?Next, you suggest a time and you say, let's meet at four.
?Then, you agree on a place and you say, we're meeting at the cafe downtown.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

I'm meeting Lucas at four o'clock.
Vou encontrar o Lucas às quatro horas.
Let's meet up at the cafe.
Vamos nos encontrar no café.
Are you free on Saturday?
Você está livre no sábado?
Why don't we get together downtown?
Por que não nos reunimos no centro?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You have a plan to see Lucas at four. You say:
A "I'm meeting Lucas at four."
B "I meet Lucas at four."
C "I meeting Lucas at four."
You want to suggest going to the cafe. You say:
A "Let's to go to the cafe."
B "Let's go to the cafe."
C "Let's going to the cafe."
A friend asks if you can come Saturday morning, but you have your class. You say:
A "I'm free in the morning."
B "I'm busy in the morning, but free in the afternoon."
C "I am on time in the morning."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine you are making a plan with a friend for Saturday. Out loud, invite your friend, suggest a time and a place with "Let's" or "Why don't we", say what you are doing with "I'm meeting..." or "We're going...", and remind them to be on time. Use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 19

1I'm meeting Lucas at four o'clock.Vou encontrar o Lucas às quatro horas.
2Let's meet up at the cafe.Vamos nos encontrar no café.
3Are you free on Saturday?Você está livre no sábado?
4Why don't we get together downtown?Por que não nos reunimos no centro?
5I'm looking forward to it. Please be on time.Mal posso esperar. Por favor, chegue na hora.
Aula 20 -- Pre-class

My Paris Trip So Far

Looking back at the whole trip: trip vocabulary (highlight, memory, experience, unforgettable) and the language to talk about your experiences with the present perfect ("I have already visited the museum", "I have not seen the tower yet", "Have you ever tried a croissant?"). New vocabulary: so far, amazing, unforgettable, highlight, memory, experience, miss, capture, journal.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary
