Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

So far -- from the beginning until now
"My trip has been amazing so far." (Até agora)
Amazing -- very good, wonderful, that surprises you in a good way
"Paris is an amazing city." (Incrível)
Unforgettable -- so special that you remember it forever
"This is an unforgettable trip." (Inesquecível)
Highlight -- the best or most special part of an event
"The highlight of my trip was the museum." (Ponto alto)
Memory -- a thing you remember from the past
"I have a beautiful memory of Paris." (Lembrança / memória)
Experience -- a thing you do or that happens to you and you learn from
"Travel is an amazing experience." (Experiência)
Miss -- to feel sad because a person or place is not with you
"I miss my family a little." (Sentir falta / sentir saudade)
Capture -- to keep a moment forever, for example in a photo
"I want to capture every memory." (Capturar / registrar)
Journal -- a book where you write about your days and feelings
"I write about my trip in my journal." (Diário)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

So far
Amazing
Unforgettable
Highlight
Memory
Experience
Miss
Capture
Journal

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela's trip to Paris has been amazing so far. It is an unforgettable experience. She has already visited a big museum and she has already seen the Eiffel Tower. She has just written three postcards to her friends. The highlight of her trip was the gift shop. She wants to capture every memory in her journal. She has not gone to the top of the tower yet, but she will go tomorrow. She misses her family a little, but this is the best trip of her life.

1. What has Gabriela already done?
A She has visited a museum and seen the tower.
B She has gone home.
C She has not done anything.
2. What has she NOT done yet?
A Written postcards.
B Gone to the top of the tower.
C Visited a museum.
3. What was the highlight of her trip?
A The airport.
B The gift shop.
C The hotel.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect (already / yet / just / ever)

GRAMMAR

Para falar de experiências até agora, usamos "have / has" + o verbo na 3a forma (visited, seen, written, been). / To talk about experiences until now, we use "have / has" + the past participle.

Use / UsoStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Done / Feito (already)have + already + verbI have already visited the museum.
Not done / Não feito (yet)have not + verb + yetI have not seen the tower yet.
A moment ago / Há pouco (just)have + just + verbI have just written a postcard.
Ask / Perguntar (ever)Have + you + ever + verb?Have you ever tried a croissant?
3rd form / 3a formavisit→visited, see→seen, go→beenwritten, done, been, seen

Key point / Ponto-chave: use have + verb (3a forma) para experiências até agora. already = já feito, yet = ainda não, just = há pouco, ever = já, na sua vida (em perguntas). Diga "I have visited" (não "I have visit").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"I have visited the museum."
"I have not seen the tower ."
"Have you traveled abroad?"
"The of my trip was the museum."
"I want to capture every ."
"This is an unforgettable ."

Stage 2: Put the Trip Recap Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de contar a sua viagem na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, you say what you have not done yet, like, I have not seen the tower yet.
?First, you think about the highlights of your trip.
?Finally, you share your favorite memory of the trip.
?Next, you say what you have already done, like, I have already visited the museum.
?Then, you ask a friend, have you ever traveled abroad?

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

I have already visited the museum.
Eu já visitei o museu.
I have not seen the tower yet.
Eu ainda não vi a torre.
Have you ever traveled abroad?
Você já viajou para o exterior?
I want to capture every memory in my journal.
Eu quero registrar cada lembrança no meu diário.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You visited the museum this morning. You say:
A "I have already visited the museum."
B "I have already visit the museum."
C "I have museum visited already."
You have not gone to the tower. You say:
A "I have not went to the tower yet."
B "I have not gone to the tower yet."
C "I have not go to the tower already."
You want to ask a friend about a life experience. You ask:
A "Did you ever traveled abroad?"
B "Have you ever traveled abroad?"
C "Have you ever travel abroad yet?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Tell the story of your Paris trip so far. Out loud, say two things you have already done, one thing you have not done yet, the highlight of your trip, and your favorite memory. Use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 20

1I have already visited the museum.Eu já visitei o museu.
2I have not seen the tower yet.Eu ainda não vi a torre.
3Have you ever traveled abroad?Você já viajou para o exterior?
4The highlight was an unforgettable experience.O ponto alto foi uma experiência inesquecível.
5I want to capture every memory in my journal.Eu quero registrar cada lembrança no meu diário.

IN CLASS

Selecione a aula para entrar no modo de slides interativos.

Lesson 01
Who Is Gabriela?
Diagnostic + Personal Introduction -- to be + personal info -- 60 min -- 28 slides
→
Lesson 02
Hello, Nice to Meet You!
Greetings + Alphabet + Hotel Check-in -- 60 min -- 27 slides
→
Lesson 03
This Is My World
Demonstratives + Articles + School Tour -- 60 min -- 30 slides
→
Lesson 04
My Daily Life
Present simple + Frequency adverbs + Routines -- 60 min -- 30 slides
→
Lesson 05
Do You Like It?
Likes, dislikes + Pop Culture -- like/love/hate + verb-ing -- 60 min -- 31 slides
→
Lesson 06
Numbers, Time & Dates
Telling time + dates + prices — Paris survival — 60 min — 29 slides
→
Lesson 07
Where Are You From?
Countries, nationalities & languages — hostel life — 60 min — 29 slides
→
Lesson 08
Can You Do That?
Abilities, asking for help & survival English — Paris emergency — 60 min — 29 slides
→
Lesson 09
Where Is It?
Directions, locations & getting around Paris — prepositions + imperatives — 60 min — 29 slides
→
Lesson 10
What Do You Look Like?
Describing people — descriptive adjectives + has/is — 60 min — 29 slides
→
Lesson 11
Let's Eat!
Food, menus & ordering at a restaurant — would like + countable/uncountable — 60 min — 28 slides
→
Lesson 12
Shopping in the City
Shopping for clothes in Paris — how much / how many + comparative adjectives — 60 min — 28 slides
→
Lesson 13
Getting Around the City
Public transport and asking for directions in Paris — imperatives + Where is / How do I get to — 60 min — 28 slides
→
Lesson 14
Eating Out Like a Local
Ordering a dinner at a restaurant in Paris — I would like / Could I have + Can you recommend — 60 min — 28 slides
→
Lesson 15
A Day at the Museum
Buying tickets and talking about art in Paris — How much is / How much are + There is / There are — 60 min — 28 slides
→
Lesson 16
Asking About the City
A guided tour in Paris and getting information — Can you tell me...? + Is there...? — 60 min — 28 slides
→
Lesson 17
Buying Gifts & Making Choices
Shopping for gifts in a Paris gift shop — choosing with one / ones + Which one...? — 60 min — 28 slides
→
Lesson 18
Sending a Postcard
At the post office in Paris — writing a short postcard and sending it with object pronouns (send it to her) — 60 min — 28 slides
→
Lesson 19
Making Plans with Friends
Arranging to meet up with friends in Paris — present continuous for future + Let's / Why don't we — 60 min — 28 slides
→
Lesson 20
My Paris Trip So Far
Looking back at the whole trip — present perfect with already / yet / just / ever — 60 min — 28 slides
→

Slides das aulas 11-48 serão adicionados nos próximos blocos.

Atividades Complementares

Séries, filmes e canais para acelerar seu progresso entre as aulas.

Series Teen — Conectadas com as Aulas

Series escolhidas para reforcar o vocabulario de cada bloco de aulas. Assista com legendas em ingles.

Lesson 11 — Let's Eat! -- Food, Menus & Ordering at a Restaurant

Series
Emily in Paris -- restaurant and cafe scenes (Season 1)

Watch the cafe and restaurant scenes. Notice how people read the menu, talk to the waiter, and say "I would like..." to order food and a drink. Listen for dessert and bill at the end.

Tip: Write down five food words you hear and one sentence with "I would like".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Ordering Food at a Restaurant"

A short, clear episode about ordering food in English: greeting the waiter, choosing a dish, asking for a drink and dessert, and asking for the bill at the end.

Tip: Record yourself ordering a full meal: a drink, a main dish, and a dessert. Use "I would like".

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "some, any and countable / uncountable nouns"

A focused lesson on countable and uncountable food words and how to use some and any. It reinforces today's grammar for talking about food and drinks at a restaurant.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences about a meal: three with some and three with any.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 12 — Shopping in the City -- Clothes, Prices & Choices

Series
Emily in Paris -- shopping and boutique scenes (Season 1)

Watch the shopping scenes in Paris. Notice how people ask "How much is it?", talk about the size and price, and compare two items with cheaper and more expensive before they choose.

Tip: Write down five shop words you hear and one comparative sentence (for example, with cheaper or more expensive).

Watch on YouTube ↗

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Shopping for Clothes"

A short, clear episode about shopping for clothes in English: asking the price, talking about sizes, trying clothes on in the fitting room, and paying at the end.

Tip: Record yourself comparing two items: one cheaper and one more expensive. Then choose one and say why.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "Comparative adjectives"

A focused lesson on comparative adjectives: how to add -er to short words (cheaper, bigger) and use more with long words (more expensive). It reinforces today's grammar for comparing clothes and prices.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences comparing clothes: three with -er and three with more.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 13 — Getting Around the City -- Transport & Directions

Series
Emily in Paris -- moving around the city scenes (Season 1)

Watch the scenes where Emily takes the metro and walks around Paris. Notice how people ask for directions with "Where is...?" and "How do I get to...?", and how they give directions with go straight and turn left.

Tip: Write down five city words you hear and one direction sentence with an imperative (for example, with go straight or turn left).

Watch on YouTube ↗

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Asking for and Giving Directions"

A short, clear episode about getting around a city in English: asking the way, taking the subway and the bus, and following directions to find a place.

Tip: Record yourself giving directions from your home to your school: go straight, turn left or right, and get off at a stop.

Listen on Culips ↗

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "Asking for directions"

A focused lesson on directions: how to ask "Where is...?" and "How do I get to...?", and how to give directions with the imperative (go straight, turn left, turn right). It reinforces today's grammar for finding your way.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences: three questions to ask the way, and three directions with go straight, turn left or get off.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 14 — Eating Out Like a Local -- Ordering & Food

Series
Emily in Paris -- restaurant and dinner scenes (Season 1)

Watch the scenes where the characters eat out in Paris. Notice how people order food with "I would like" and "Could I have", and how they ask the waiter to recommend a dish.

Tip: Write down five restaurant words you hear and one polite order with "I would like" or "Could I have".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Ordering Food at a Restaurant"

A short, clear episode about eating out in English: booking a table, asking the waiter to recommend a dish, ordering an appetizer and a main course, and paying the check.

Tip: Record yourself ordering a full dinner: an appetizer, a main course, and ask for the check at the end.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "At a restaurant"

A focused lesson on eating out: how to order politely with "I would like" and "Could I have", and how to ask the waiter to recommend something. It reinforces today's polite ordering language.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences: three polite orders with "I would like", and three requests with "Could I have" or "Can you recommend".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 15 — A Day at the Museum -- Tickets & Art

Series
Night at the Museum -- the museum tour scenes

Watch the scenes inside the museum. Notice the words for the galleries, paintings and sculptures, and how the characters describe what they see with "There is" and "There are".

Tip: Write down five museum words you hear and one sentence with "There is" and one with "There are".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Visiting a Museum"

A short, clear episode about a day at a museum in English: buying a ticket, asking how much it costs, finding the entrance, and talking about the exhibition and the art inside.

Tip: Record yourself buying a ticket and describing a gallery: ask "How much is", then use "There is" and "There are".

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "At the museum"

A focused lesson on visiting a museum: how to ask prices with "How much is" and "How much are", and how to describe an exhibition with "There is" and "There are". It reinforces today's grammar.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences: two questions with "How much is/are", and four descriptions with "There is/are".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 16 — Asking About the City -- A Guided Tour & Getting Information

Series
Emily in Paris -- the sightseeing scenes

Watch the scenes where Emily explores Paris and visits the landmarks. Notice how people ask for information and talk about the tour, the view, and the places nearby.

Tip: Write down five tour words you hear and one question that starts with "Can you tell me".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Asking for Information"

A short, clear episode about asking for information politely in English: how to start with "Can you tell me", how to ask if something exists with "Is there", and how to talk about a tour.

Tip: Record yourself asking three polite questions on a city tour: use "Can you tell me" twice and "Is there" once.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "Asking for information"

A focused lesson on getting information politely: how to use "Can you tell me..." with where, what time and how long, and how to ask "Is there...?" It reinforces today's grammar.

Tip: After watching, write six questions: four with "Can you tell me" and two with "Is there".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 17 — Buying Gifts & Making Choices

Series
Gilmore Girls -- the gift shopping scenes

Watch the scenes where the characters buy presents in a store. Notice how they choose between things with "one" and "ones" and ask "Which one?".

Tip: Write down five gift words you hear and one sentence with "I'll take the ... one".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Shopping for Gifts"

A short, clear episode about buying gifts in English: how to choose with "one" and "ones", how to ask "Which one?", and how to say "I'll take..." in a shop.

Tip: Record yourself buying three gifts: use "one" once, "ones" once, and "I'll take" once.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "one and ones"

A focused lesson on using "one" and "ones" to avoid repeating the noun, plus "Which one?" for making choices. It reinforces today's grammar.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences: three with "one" and three with "ones".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 18 — Sending a Postcard

Series
Emily in Paris -- the letter and message scenes

Watch a scene where a character writes a message or sends mail. Notice how people use short words like "it", "them", "her" and "him" instead of repeating the name.

Tip: Write down three sentences you hear with "it" or "them", and say who each one is about.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "At the Post Office"

A short, clear episode about sending mail in English: how to ask for a stamp, talk about sending things abroad by air mail, and use "send it to her / him".

Tip: Record yourself sending three things: use "it" once, "them" once, and "her" or "him" once.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "object pronouns: it, them, her, him"

A focused lesson on object pronouns: when to use "it" for one thing, "them" for many, "her" for a woman and "him" for a man. It reinforces today's grammar.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences about sending mail, one for each: it, them, her, him.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 19 — Making Plans with Friends

Series
Heartstopper -- the friends making weekend plans

Watch the scenes where the teenagers arrange to meet up and hang out. Notice how they say "Are you free?", "Let's meet up" and "I'm meeting...".

Tip: Write down three sentences they use to make a plan, with a time and a place.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Making Plans with Friends"

A short, clear episode about arranging to meet up in English: how to ask "Are you free?", how to suggest with "Let's" and "Why don't we", and how to talk about a plan with "I'm meeting...".

Tip: Record yourself making a plan: use "I'm meeting" once, "Let's" once, and "on time" once.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "Present continuous for future plans"

A focused lesson on using "I'm meeting" and "We're going" to talk about arranged future plans, plus "Let's" and "Why don't we" for suggestions. It reinforces today's grammar.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences about your weekend plans with "be + verb-ing".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 20 — My Paris Trip So Far

Series
Emily in Paris -- the travel moments

Watch the scenes where characters talk about what they have done and seen in Paris. Notice the present perfect: "I have visited...", "Have you ever...?".

Tip: Write down three sentences you hear with "have" + verb, then say one about your own week.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Talking About Experiences"

A short, clear episode about the present perfect: how to use "already", "yet", "just" and "ever" to talk about what you have done in your life and on a trip.

Tip: Record yourself saying three things you have already done today and one thing you have not done yet.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "Present Perfect: already, yet, just"

A focused lesson on the present perfect with "already", "yet" and "just", plus "ever" for life experiences. It reinforces today's grammar.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences about your trip or week: two with "already", two with "yet", two with "just".

Watch on YouTube ↗
